NKE.reasoning: Catalyst window: No identifiable catalyst within 10 trading days. Q4 FY2026 earnings not until June 25, 2026 (~37 trading days out). Net news sentiment: Negative — all April 2026 headlines are adverse (52-week low hit, Piper Sandler/DA Davidson/Citigroup downgrades, CIO departure after <1 year, Q3 revenues flat with Nike Direct -7% currency-neutral, EPS -43.5% YoY). No recent news in current facts bundle. Insider activity: Conflicted — meaningful board-level buying ($500K and $173K in early April at multi-year lows) is a contrarian signal, but 90-trade net flow remains sell-side heavy ($19.7M buys vs. $33.2M sells). Technical setup going in: Poor for catalyst trade — full EMA aligned downtrend, price 14.28% below 50 SMA and 30% below 200 SMA, daily OBV trend down, hourly OBV diverging bearishly from price. The stock is deeply oversold (RSI-7: 15.36, RSI-21: 28.53) but there is no constructive base or coiling pattern — just a persistent downtrend. Without a catalyst within 10 trading days and with the technical setup failing the constructive-base test, this is a hold per the catalyst-news strategy rules. The June 25 earnings date is a future binary event to monitor; re-evaluate ~June 12–15 once inside the 10-day catalyst window.
NKE.timeframe: no trade — revisit June 12-15 for earnings catalyst window

DIS.reasoning: Catalyst window: Q2 FY2026 earnings approximately May 6–13, 2026 — roughly 4–9 trading days away, firmly inside the catalyst window. This is the primary thesis driver. Net news sentiment: Positive lean — streaming bundle expansion (Disney+/Hulu/Max), Disney-Epic Games partnership, and restructuring cost cuts read as margin-positive. The April 2026 job cuts (1,000 roles including Marvel) carry a modest content-quality risk flag from prior wiki analysis, keeping sentiment mixed-to-positive rather than strongly positive. Insider activity: Net buying — 88 buys vs. 71 sells in April 2026, with board RSU grants near $96.96 establishing institutional cost basis below current price. Management is signaling conviction ahead of the earnings print. Technical setup going in: Constructive — price above all short-term MAs (5/10/20/21/50 EMA and SMA), hourly EMAs aligned uptrend, daily ADX 37.34 with +DI 32.31 well above -DI 18.74 (strong bullish trend), daily MACD bullish crossover confirmed. April 30 produced a strong bullish candle (+$2.45) on elevated volume (9.19M vs. 7.48M 20-day avg), reversing from intraday low ~$100.42. Hourly relative volume 1.23 with 10-day volume bias up — informed pre-catalyst accumulation pattern. Z-score 0.75 (not extended). Risk: Analyst consensus Buy at $132–140 avg PT implies significant upside, but current price ~$104 is below 200 SMA ($109.33), and the Paramount-WBD merger regulatory decision (if cleared) creates a competitive overhang. Earnings miss vs. $1.57 EPS / $25.76B revenue estimates would invalidate the setup — stop below $101.02 pre-catalyst support (volume-confirmed, 17 tests). Lessons check: Recent catalyst loss was NVDA EMA pullback (different setup type, different name), no direct read-through. Confidence capped at 68 due to earnings binary risk and daily OBV lagging.
